back_translation: It was when Samuel was old, that he set his sons as judges over Israel.
The name of his firstborn was Joel, and the name of his second was Abijah — judges in Beersheba.
His sons did not walk in his ways, and they turned aside after gain, and they took bribes, and they turned aside justice.
All the elders of Israel gathered themselves together, and they came to Samuel at Ramah.
They said to him, 'Behold, you are old, and your sons do not walk in your ways — now appoint for us a king to judge us, like all the nations.'
The thing was evil in the eyes of Samuel when they said, 'Give us a king to judge us,' and Samuel prayed to the LORD.
The LORD said to Samuel, 'Listen to the voice of the people in all that they say to you, because not you have they rejected — but me they have rejected from reigning over them.'
'Like all the deeds that they did from the day I brought them up from Egypt until this day — and they have forsaken me and served other gods — so they are doing also to you.'
'And now, listen to their voice; only warning shall you warn against them, and you shall declare to them the custom of the king who will reign over them.'
Samuel told all the words of the LORD to the people who were asking from him a king.
He said, 'This will be the custom of the king who will reign over you: your sons he will take and set for himself in his chariot and his horsemen, and they shall run before his chariot.'
'And to set for himself commanders of thousands and commanders of fifties, and to plow his plowing, and to reap his harvest, and to make his weapons of war and his chariot equipment.'
'And your daughters he will take as perfumers and cooks and bakers.'
'And your fields and your vineyards and your olive groves, the best, he will take and give to his servants.'
'And your seed and your vineyards he will tithe and give to his officials and to his servants.'
'And your menservants and your maidservants, and your best young men, and your donkeys he will take and use for his work.'
'Your flocks he will tithe — and you shall be slaves to him.'
'You shall cry out on that day from before your king whom you have chosen for yourselves, but the LORD will not answer you on that day.'
The people refused to listen to the voice of Samuel, and they said, 'No — but a king shall be over us.'
'And we shall be, also we, like all the nations — and our king shall judge us, and he shall go out before us, and he shall fight our wars.'
Samuel heard all the words of the people, and he spoke them in the ears of the LORD.
The LORD said to Samuel, 'Listen to their voice, and appoint for them a king.' And Samuel said to the men of Israel, 'Go each man to his city.'
